dublinCore.dc:title: 119 SJ 112 IS: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce relating to “One Year Suspension of Expansion of End-User Controls for Affiliates of Certain Listed Entities”.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Warren (for herself, Mr. Kim, Mr. Wyden, Ms. Cortez Masto, Mr. Kaine, Mr. Merkley, and Mr. Coons) introduced the following joint resolution; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce relating to One Year Suspension of Expansion of End-User Controls for Affiliates of Certain Listed Entities.

section.text: That Congress disapproves the rule submitted by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the Department of Commerce relating to One Year Suspension of Expansion of End-User Controls for Affiliates of Certain Listed Entities (90 Fed. Reg. 50857 (November 12, 2025)), and such rule shall have no force or effect.
